summary: Scorching second set with Shakedown Street, Ship of Fools & soaring Morning Dew
blurb: The June 13 ’87 show delivers a blistering second set highlighted by Shakedown Street, Ship Of Fools, Estimated Prophet into Eyes Of The World, and an epic Morning Dew. Jerry’s guitar is on fire and Brent supplies punchy piano/keys throughout. Despite only fair sound quality, the performance consistently earns high praise across recordings.
review: Ventura County Fairgrounds saw the Dead at a peak of summer heat on June 13 1987, and the band responded with an incendiary second set that still sparks conversation among fans. Opening the night with solid staples like Mississippi Half‑Step and Walkin’ Blues, they built momentum into a scorching Shakedown Street that launched a string of highlights: a soulful Ship Of Fools, a seamless Estimated Prophet → Eyes Of The World transition, and a thunderous drum‑>Space → The Other One journey that culminated in one of the most soaring Morning Dew jams of the era. Jerry Garcia’s vocals cut straight through the mix while his guitar roared with excitement, especially on Ship Of Fools and the climactic Dew. Brent Mydland’s piano anchored the first set and added tasteful synth textures, though a few recordings note occasional thinness in the mix. The dual drumming of Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann kept the groove tight and the space segment “awesome,” driving the energy that makes this show a fan favorite despite the modest recording fidelity.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All four analyses carry a positive sentiment, repeatedly praising the second‑set firepower (Shakedown Street, Ship Of Fools, Estimated Prophet, Morning Dew) and Jerry’s excitable guitar work. Multiple recordings call the show "legendary," "scorching," and "barn‑burner," indicating strong consensus of exceptional performance across sources.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source with the clearest overall mix, capturing the full set and the band’s hot energy despite occasional strain.

key_highlights: Second‑set blaze from Shakedown Street through Ship Of Fools into a seamless Estimated Prophet → Eyes Of The World transition.
Epic Morning Dew jam that builds to a soaring climax.
Jerry’s powerful guitar and vocal intensity drive the performance, complemented by Brent’s punchy piano/keys.
Recording quality is fair but soundboard captures the band’s energy; it remains the most listenable source.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia is visibly excited, delivering powerful guitar work and impassioned vocals, especially on Ship Of Fools, Morning Dew and the high‑energy second‑set jams.
band_performance.Brent: Brent’s piano drives the first set with solid comping and standout solos on Row Jimmy and Walkin’ Blues; his synth textures add flavor though a few recordings note the mix can sound thin at times.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart’s drumming locks in tight grooves, propelling the second‑set flow and contributing to the dynamic drum‑>Space segment that fans describe as awesome.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann matches Hart’s energy, providing steady power and rhythmic drive throughout the set, especially during the extended jams on The Other One and Morning Dew.
